cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. So I need to figure out the correct answer based on the passage. The question is asking what we can infer from the passage. The options are A to D. First, let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. The passage talks about the history of volleyball starting in 1895 with William G. Morgan. He was influenced by basketball and wanted a slower game for middle-aged men. He created "mintonette," which later became volleyball. The game was similar to badminton but used hands instead of rackets to cut down equipment costs. It became popular, spread, and rules were set over time. The rules included court size, number of players, rotation for serving, and standard ball size. The rules changed over time until the game became what it is today. Now, the game is popular among all age groups and various teams. Now, the question is to infer which option is correct. Let's look at the options: A) Volleyball develops from the game of "mintonette". The passage says that Morgan created "mintonette," which became volleyball. So this seems directly stated, not an inference. The passage says "the game of mintonette" and later refers to it as volleyball. So A is probably correct, but the question is about an inference. Wait, maybe the answer is in the options. B) Most of the competitive volleyball teams are in the college. The passage mentions high school, college, women, men, children, mixed teams, etc. But it says "there are many different kinds..." but doesn't specify that most are in college. So this is not supported. The passage doesn't give numbers or a majority, so B is probably incorrect. C) The rules of volleyball continue to change as they are easy to learn. The passage says the rules continued to change until the game became what it is today. The reason for the changes is not stated here. It also mentions that the game is easy to learn, but does it link the changing of rules to the ease of learning? The passage says "it can be played indoors or outside" and is easy to learn, but the rules changing is due to the game's development. So C
